[[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[sn:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2740324]] Wikidata name: Noriko Senge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Former Japanese princess (born 1988)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2021}} {{Eastern name order|Senge Noriko}} {{Infobox person |name = Noriko Senge |image = File:Princess Noriko 2013.JPG |caption = Noriko in 2013 |birth_name = {{nihongo|Princess Noriko of Takamado|典子女王}} |birth_date = {{birth date|1988|7|22}} |birth_place = {{ill|Aiiku Hospital|WD=Q11073872}} |spouse = {{ill|Kunimaro Senge|WD=Q16963459}} |father = {{ill|Norihito, Prince Takamado|WD=Q10856511}} |mother = {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104}} |relatives = {{ill|Ayako Moriya|WD=Q2379776}}; {{ill|Princess Tsuguko of Takamado|WD=Q2727922}} |gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} |nationality= {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} |occupation= {{ill|aristocrat|WD=Q2478141}} |family= {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452}} |awards= {{ill|The Order of the Precious Crown, Peony|WD=Q64735711}} |religion= {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |native_name= 千家典子 |given_name= {{ill|Noriko|WD=Q7051261}} }} {{nihongo|'''Noriko Senge'''|千家 典子|Senge Noriko|extra= born 22 July 1988}}, formerly {{nihongo|'''Princess Noriko of Takamado'''|典子女王|Noriko Joō}}, is a former member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] and the second daughter of [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] and [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako, Princess Takamado]].{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/asia-pacific/2526599.stm|title=Funeral held for Japan's prince|date=29 November 2002|work=[[BBC Online]]|accessdate=28 April 2011}} She married Kunimaro Senge, a commoner, on 5 October 2014. As a result, she gave up her imperial title and left the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]], as required by law. ==Biography== [[File:Cephalanthera falcata 1.jpg|thumb|right|upright|[[:en:orchid|orchid]]s, designated imperial personal emblem of Noriko]] Princess Noriko was born on 22 July 1988 at Aiiku Hospital in [[:en:Minami-Azabu|Minami-Azabu]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. After her birth, her father, Prince Takamado, chose her name. She went to Gakushuin Kindergarten. After that she attended the prestigious [[:en:Gakushūin|Gakushuin]] School for her primary, junior high, and high school education. In April 2007, Princess Noriko enrolled in the [[:en:Gakushuin University|Gakushuin University]], Faculty of Letters, Department of Psychology, and expressed her intent to pursue studies in the field of [[:en:clinical psychology|clinical psychology]].{{cite web |title=Personal History of Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family |url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/history/history08.html |publisher=[[Imperial Household Agency]] |date=|accessdate=19 May 2012}} In July 2008, she came of age and attended official ceremonies in Japan with the other members of the Imperial Family. She attended official functions at the palace and accompanied her mother to some other official events, as well as attending some on her own. On 20 March 2011, she graduated from Gakushuin University with the [[:en:Bachelor of Arts|B.A.]] degree in [[:en:Psychology|Psychology]]. ===Marriage=== On 27 May 2014, Princess Noriko announced her engagement to {{nihongo|Kunimaro Senge|千家国麿|Senge Kunimaro|extra= born {{Birth date|1973|9|2|df=y}}}}, a [[kannushi]] for the [[Izumo-taisha]] shrine.{{cite news|title=Wedding Bells to Ring for Japanese Princess|url=https://blogs.wsj.com/japanrealtime/2014/05/27/wedding-bells-to-ring-for-japanese-princess/|newspaper=Wall Street Journal Japan}} The two married on 5 October at the shrine.{{cite web|title=Princess Noriko marries son of Izumo Taisha priest, relinquishes royal status|date=5 October 2014 |url=http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/10/05/national/princess-noriko-marries-son-of-izumo-taisha-priest-relinquishes-royal-status/#.VDFoc1e5_y0|accessdate=5 October 2014}}{{cite news|title=Marriage of Her Imperial Highness Princess Noriko of Takamado - Wedding Ceremony at the Izumo Oyasahiro Shrine in Shimane Prefecture|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/gokekkon-h26/e-kekkonsiki-ph.html|work=The Imperial Household Agency|accessdate=16 May 2015}} Senge Kunimaro is the eldest son of Senge Takamasa, the head priest of the Izumo Grand Shrine. The [[:en:Senge family|Senge family]] have been hereditary administrators of the shrine for generations. Though legally commoners today, the heads of the family held the title of [[:en:Baron|Baron]] in the Imperial Japanese peerage (''[[kazoku]]'') before its abolition in 1947. Senge Kunimaro is a second cousin (twice removed) of the present Emperor and a distant cousin twice over of Princess Noriko. He and Noriko are third cousins once removed by their shared descent from Prince [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Kujō Michitaka]], who was the father of the Empress Teimei, Princess Noriko's great-grandmother and Senge Kunimaro's great-great-aunt.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%8d%83%e5%ae%b6%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%87%ba%e9%9b%b2%e5%9b%bd%e9%80%a0%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#kunimaro973|title=千家家（Izumo Province 造家） (Senge genealogy)|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} The couple are also fifth cousins through their shared descent from Marquis [[:en:Nakayama Tadayasu|Nakayama Tadayasu]], the maternal grandfather of the [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]].{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e4%b8%ad%e5%b1%b1%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e7%be%bd%e6%9e%97%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#suketikaq|script-title=ja:中山家（羽林家）|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} By birth, Kunimaro is also related to other members of the imperial family. He is a sixth cousin once removed of his grandmother-in-law, [[:en:Princess Mikasa|Princess Mikasa]], a third cousin twice removed of [[:en:Princess Hitachi|Princess Hitachi]] and a cousin of the late Princesses [[:en:Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] and [[:en:Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|Chichibu]] (a second cousin thrice removed and a fourth cousin thrice removed, respectively). After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and [[:en:allowance (money)|allowance]] from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]] to marry a commoner since the passage of the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of [[:en:Sayako Kuroda|Sayako, Princess Nori]], [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Akihito's]] daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle=[[:en:Imperial Highness|Her Imperial Highness]] | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] |3= 3. [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako Tottori]] |4= 4. [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Takahito, Prince Mikasa]] |5= 5. [[:en:Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|The Hon. Yuriko Takagi]] |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō]] |9= 9. [[:en:Empress Teimei|Lady Sadako Kujō]] |10= 10. Masanari Takagi |11= 11. Kuniko Irie |12= 12. Tamesaburo Tottori |13= 13. Hisashi Tottori |14= 14. Jiro Tomoda |15= 15. Moriko Tomoda |16= 16. [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Mutsuhito, Emperor Meiji]] |17= 17. [[:en:Yanagihara Naruko|Lady Naruko Yanagihara]] |18= 18. [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Prince Kujō Michitaka]] |19= 19. Noma Ikuko |20= 20. Masayoshi Takagi |21= 21. Saneko Matsudaira |22= 22. Tamemori Irie |23= 23. Nobuko Yanagihara }} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Senge Noriko}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/activity08.html Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family] at the Imperial Household Agency website {{Japanese princesses}} {{Izumo-taishakyo}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Nobility from Tokyo]] [[Category:People from Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gakushuin University alumni]] [[Category:Order of the Precious Crown members]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] }} [[en:Noriko Senge]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]] [[azb:نوریکو سنقه]] {{Commons category|Senge Noriko}} [[es:Noriko Senge]] [[fa:سنگه نوریکو]] [[fr:Noriko Senge]] [[gn:Noriko Senge]] [[id:Noriko Senge]] [[it:Noriko Senge]] [[ja:千家典子]] [[ko:센게 노리코]] [[pl:Noriko Senge]] [[pt:Noriko Senge]] [[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]] [[vi:Senge Noriko]] [[zh:千家典子]] {{wikidata link|Q2740324}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Princess Tsuguko of Takamado|WD=Q2727922}} |gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} |nationality= {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} |occupation= {{ill|aristocrat|WD=Q2478141}} |family= {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452}} |awards= {{ill|The Order of the Precious Crown, Peony|WD=Q64735711}} |religion= {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |native_name= 千家典子 |given_name= {{ill|Noriko|WD=Q7051261}} }} {{nihongo|'''Noriko Senge'''|千家 典子|Senge Noriko|extra= born 22 July 1988}}, formerly {{nihongo|'''Princess Noriko of Takamado'''|典子女王|Noriko Joō}}, is a former member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] and the second daughter of [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] and [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako, Princess Takamado]].{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/asia-pacific/2526599.stm|title=Funeral held for Japan's prince|date=29 November 2002|work=[[BBC Online]]|accessdate=28 April 2011}} She married Kunimaro Senge, a commoner, on 5 October 2014. As a result, she gave up her imperial title and left the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]], as required by law. ==Biography== [[File:Cephalanthera falcata 1.jpg|thumb|right|upright|[[:en:orchid|orchid]]s, designated imperial personal emblem of Noriko]] Princess Noriko was born on 22 July 1988 at Aiiku Hospital in [[:en:Minami-Azabu|Minami-Azabu]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. After her birth, her father, Prince Takamado, chose her name. She went to Gakushuin Kindergarten. After that she attended the prestigious [[:en:Gakushūin|Gakushuin]] School for her primary, junior high, and high school education. In April 2007, Princess Noriko enrolled in the [[:en:Gakushuin University|Gakushuin University]], Faculty of Letters, Department of Psychology, and expressed her intent to pursue studies in the field of [[:en:clinical psychology|clinical psychology]].{{cite web |title=Personal History of Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family |url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/history/history08.html |publisher=[[Imperial Household Agency]] |date=|accessdate=19 May 2012}} In July 2008, she came of age and attended official ceremonies in Japan with the other members of the Imperial Family. She attended official functions at the palace and accompanied her mother to some other official events, as well as attending some on her own. On 20 March 2011, she graduated from Gakushuin University with the [[:en:Bachelor of Arts|B.A.]] degree in [[:en:Psychology|Psychology]]. ===Marriage=== On 27 May 2014, Princess Noriko announced her engagement to {{nihongo|Kunimaro Senge|千家国麿|Senge Kunimaro|extra= born {{Birth date|1973|9|2|df=y}}}}, a [[kannushi]] for the [[Izumo-taisha]] shrine.{{cite news|title=Wedding Bells to Ring for Japanese Princess|url=https://blogs.wsj.com/japanrealtime/2014/05/27/wedding-bells-to-ring-for-japanese-princess/|newspaper=Wall Street Journal Japan}} The two married on 5 October at the shrine.{{cite web|title=Princess Noriko marries son of Izumo Taisha priest, relinquishes royal status|date=5 October 2014 |url=http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/10/05/national/princess-noriko-marries-son-of-izumo-taisha-priest-relinquishes-royal-status/#.VDFoc1e5_y0|accessdate=5 October 2014}}{{cite news|title=Marriage of Her Imperial Highness Princess Noriko of Takamado - Wedding Ceremony at the Izumo Oyasahiro Shrine in Shimane Prefecture|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/gokekkon-h26/e-kekkonsiki-ph.html|work=The Imperial Household Agency|accessdate=16 May 2015}} Senge Kunimaro is the eldest son of Senge Takamasa, the head priest of the Izumo Grand Shrine. The [[:en:Senge family|Senge family]] have been hereditary administrators of the shrine for generations. Though legally commoners today, the heads of the family held the title of [[:en:Baron|Baron]] in the Imperial Japanese peerage (''[[kazoku]]'') before its abolition in 1947. Senge Kunimaro is a second cousin (twice removed) of the present Emperor and a distant cousin twice over of Princess Noriko. He and Noriko are third cousins once removed by their shared descent from Prince [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Kujō Michitaka]], who was the father of the Empress Teimei, Princess Noriko's great-grandmother and Senge Kunimaro's great-great-aunt.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%8d%83%e5%ae%b6%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%87%ba%e9%9b%b2%e5%9b%bd%e9%80%a0%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#kunimaro973|title=千家家（Izumo Province 造家） (Senge genealogy)|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} The couple are also fifth cousins through their shared descent from Marquis [[:en:Nakayama Tadayasu|Nakayama Tadayasu]], the maternal grandfather of the [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]].{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e4%b8%ad%e5%b1%b1%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e7%be%bd%e6%9e%97%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#suketikaq|script-title=ja:中山家（羽林家）|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} By birth, Kunimaro is also related to other members of the imperial family. He is a sixth cousin once removed of his grandmother-in-law, [[:en:Princess Mikasa|Princess Mikasa]], a third cousin twice removed of [[:en:Princess Hitachi|Princess Hitachi]] and a cousin of the late Princesses [[:en:Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] and [[:en:Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|Chichibu]] (a second cousin thrice removed and a fourth cousin thrice removed, respectively). After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and [[:en:allowance (money)|allowance]] from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]] to marry a commoner since the passage of the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of [[:en:Sayako Kuroda|Sayako, Princess Nori]], [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Akihito's]] daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle=[[:en:Imperial Highness|Her Imperial Highness]] | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] |3= 3. [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako Tottori]] |4= 4. [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Takahito, Prince Mikasa]] |5= 5. [[:en:Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|The Hon. Yuriko Takagi]] |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō]] |9= 9. [[:en:Empress Teimei|Lady Sadako Kujō]] |10= 10. Masanari Takagi |11= 11. Kuniko Irie |12= 12. Tamesaburo Tottori |13= 13. Hisashi Tottori |14= 14. Jiro Tomoda |15= 15. Moriko Tomoda |16= 16. [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Mutsuhito, Emperor Meiji]] |17= 17. [[:en:Yanagihara Naruko|Lady Naruko Yanagihara]] |18= 18. [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Prince Kujō Michitaka]] |19= 19. Noma Ikuko |20= 20. Masayoshi Takagi |21= 21. Saneko Matsudaira |22= 22. Tamemori Irie |23= 23. Nobuko Yanagihara }} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Senge Noriko}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/activity08.html Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family] at the Imperial Household Agency website {{Japanese princesses}} {{Izumo-taishakyo}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Nobility from Tokyo]] [[Category:People from Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Gakushuin University alumni]] [[Category:Order of the Precious Crown members]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] }} [[en:Noriko Senge]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]] [[azb:نوریکو سنقه]] {{Commons category|Senge Noriko}} [[es:Noriko Senge]] [[fa:سنگه نوریکو]] [[fr:Noriko Senge]] [[gn:Noriko Senge]] [[id:Noriko Senge]] [[it:Noriko Senge]] [[ja:千家典子]] [[ko:센게 노리코]] [[pl:Noriko Senge]] [[pt:Noriko Senge]] [[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]] [[vi:Senge Noriko]] [[zh:千家典子]] {{wikidata link|Q2740324}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[sn:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2740324]] Wikidata name: Noriko Senge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Former Japanese princess (born 1988)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2021}} {{Eastern name order|Senge Noriko}} {{Infobox person |name = Noriko Senge |image = File:Princess Noriko 2013.JPG |caption = Noriko in 2013 |birth_name = {{nihongo|Princess Noriko of Takamado|典子女王}} |birth_date = {{birth date|1988|7|22}} |birth_place = {{ill|Aiiku Hospital|WD=Q11073872}} |spouse = {{ill|Kunimaro Senge|WD=Q16963459}} |father = {{ill|Norihito, Prince Takamado|WD=Q10856511}} |mother = {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104}} |relatives = {{ill|Ayako Moriya|WD=Q2379776}}; {{ill|Princess Tsuguko of Takamado|WD=Q2727922}} |gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} |nationality= {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} |occupation= {{ill|aristocrat|WD=Q2478141}} |family= {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452}} |awards= {{ill|The Order of the Precious Crown, Peony|WD=Q64735711}} |religion= {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |native_name= 千家典子 |given_name= {{ill|Noriko|WD=Q7051261}} }} {{nihongo|'''Noriko Senge'''|千家 典子|Senge Noriko|extra= born 22 July 1988}}, formerly {{nihongo|'''Princess Noriko of Takamado'''|典子女王|Noriko Joō}}, is a former member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] and the second daughter of [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] and [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako, Princess Takamado]].{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/asia-pacific/2526599.stm|title=Funeral held for Japan's prince|date=29 November 2002|work=[[BBC Online]]|accessdate=28 April 2011}} She married Kunimaro Senge, a commoner, on 5 October 2014. As a result, she gave up her imperial title and left the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]], as required by law. ==Biography== [[File:Cephalanthera falcata 1.jpg|thumb|right|upright|[[:en:orchid|orchid]]s, designated imperial personal emblem of Noriko]] Princess Noriko was born on 22 July 1988 at Aiiku Hospital in [[:en:Minami-Azabu|Minami-Azabu]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. After her birth, her father, Prince Takamado, chose her name. She went to Gakushuin Kindergarten. After that she attended the prestigious [[:en:Gakushūin|Gakushuin]] School for her primary, junior high, and high school education. In April 2007, Princess Noriko enrolled in the [[:en:Gakushuin University|Gakushuin University]], Faculty of Letters, Department of Psychology, and expressed her intent to pursue studies in the field of [[:en:clinical psychology|clinical psychology]].{{cite web |title=Personal History of Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family |url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/history/history08.html |publisher=[[Imperial Household Agency]] |date=|accessdate=19 May 2012}} In July 2008, she came of age and attended official ceremonies in Japan with the other members of the Imperial Family. She attended official functions at the palace and accompanied her mother to some other official events, as well as attending some on her own. On 20 March 2011, she graduated from Gakushuin University with the [[:en:Bachelor of Arts|B.A.]] degree in [[:en:Psychology|Psychology]]. ===Marriage=== On 27 May 2014, Princess Noriko announced her engagement to {{nihongo|Kunimaro Senge|千家国麿|Senge Kunimaro|extra= born {{Birth date|1973|9|2|df=y}}}}, a [[kannushi]] for the [[Izumo-taisha]] shrine.{{cite news|title=Wedding Bells to Ring for Japanese Princess|url=https://blogs.wsj.com/japanrealtime/2014/05/27/wedding-bells-to-ring-for-japanese-princess/|newspaper=Wall Street Journal Japan}} The two married on 5 October at the shrine.{{cite web|title=Princess Noriko marries son of Izumo Taisha priest, relinquishes royal status|date=5 October 2014 |url=http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/10/05/national/princess-noriko-marries-son-of-izumo-taisha-priest-relinquishes-royal-status/#.VDFoc1e5_y0|accessdate=5 October 2014}}{{cite news|title=Marriage of Her Imperial Highness Princess Noriko of Takamado - Wedding Ceremony at the Izumo Oyasahiro Shrine in Shimane Prefecture|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/gokekkon-h26/e-kekkonsiki-ph.html|work=The Imperial Household Agency|accessdate=16 May 2015}} Senge Kunimaro is the eldest son of Senge Takamasa, the head priest of the Izumo Grand Shrine. The [[:en:Senge family|Senge family]] have been hereditary administrators of the shrine for generations. Though legally commoners today, the heads of the family held the title of [[:en:Baron|Baron]] in the Imperial Japanese peerage (''[[kazoku]]'') before its abolition in 1947. Senge Kunimaro is a second cousin (twice removed) of the present Emperor and a distant cousin twice over of Princess Noriko. He and Noriko are third cousins once removed by their shared descent from Prince [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Kujō Michitaka]], who was the father of the Empress Teimei, Princess Noriko's great-grandmother and Senge Kunimaro's great-great-aunt.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%8d%83%e5%ae%b6%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%87%ba%e9%9b%b2%e5%9b%bd%e9%80%a0%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#kunimaro973|title=千家家（Izumo Province 造家） (Senge genealogy)|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} The couple are also fifth cousins through their shared descent from Marquis [[:en:Nakayama Tadayasu|Nakayama Tadayasu]], the maternal grandfather of the [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]].{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e4%b8%ad%e5%b1%b1%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e7%be%bd%e6%9e%97%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#suketikaq|script-title=ja:中山家（羽林家）|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} By birth, Kunimaro is also related to other members of the imperial family. He is a sixth cousin once removed of his grandmother-in-law, [[:en:Princess Mikasa|Princess Mikasa]], a third cousin twice removed of [[:en:Princess Hitachi|Princess Hitachi]] and a cousin of the late Princesses [[:en:Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] and [[:en:Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|Chichibu]] (a second cousin thrice removed and a fourth cousin thrice removed, respectively). After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and [[:en:allowance (money)|allowance]] from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]] to marry a commoner since the passage of the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of [[:en:Sayako Kuroda|Sayako, Princess Nori]], [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Akihito's]] daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle=[[:en:Imperial Highness|Her Imperial Highness]] | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] |3= 3. [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako Tottori]] |4= 4. [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Takahito, Prince Mikasa]] |5= 5. [[:en:Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|The Hon. Yuriko Takagi]] |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō]] |9= 9. [[:en:Empress Teimei|Lady Sadako Kujō]] |10= 10. Masanari Takagi |11= 11. Kuniko Irie |12= 12. Tamesaburo Tottori |13= 13. Hisashi Tottori |14= 14. Jiro Tomoda |15= 15. Moriko Tomoda |16= 16. [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Mutsuhito, Emperor Meiji]] |17= 17. [[:en:Yanagihara Naruko|Lady Naruko Yanagihara]] |18= 18. [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Prince Kujō Michitaka]] |19= 19. Noma Ikuko |20= 20. Masayoshi Takagi |21= 21. Saneko Matsudaira |22= 22. Tamemori Irie |23= 23. Nobuko Yanagihara }} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Senge Noriko}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/activity08.html Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family] at the Imperial Household Agency website {{Japanese princesses}} {{Izumo-taishakyo}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Noriko Senge]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]] [[azb:نوریکو سنقه]] {{Commons category|Senge Noriko}} [[es:Noriko Senge]] [[fa:سنگه نوریکو]] [[fr:Noriko Senge]] [[gn:Noriko Senge]] [[id:Noriko Senge]] [[it:Noriko Senge]] [[ja:千家典子]] [[ko:센게 노리코]] [[pl:Noriko Senge]] [[pt:Noriko Senge]] [[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]] [[vi:Senge Noriko]] [[zh:千家典子]] {{wikidata link|Q2740324}} [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Gakushuin University alumni]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Nobility from Tokyo]] [[Category:Order of the Precious Crown members]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:People from Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[sn:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2740324]] Wikidata name: Noriko Senge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Former Japanese princess (born 1988)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2021}} {{Eastern name order|Senge Noriko}} {{Infobox person |name = Noriko Senge |image = File:Princess Noriko 2013.JPG |caption = Noriko in 2013 |birth_name = {{nihongo|Princess Noriko of Takamado|典子女王}} |birth_date = {{birth date|1988|7|22}} |birth_place = {{ill|Aiiku Hospital|WD=Q11073872}} |spouse = {{ill|Kunimaro Senge|WD=Q16963459}} |father = {{ill|Norihito, Prince Takamado|WD=Q10856511}} |mother = {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104}} |relatives = {{ill|Ayako Moriya|WD=Q2379776}}; {{ill|Princess Tsuguko of Takamado|WD=Q2727922}} |gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} |nationality= {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} |occupation= {{ill|aristocrat|WD=Q2478141}} |family= {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452}} |awards= {{ill|The Order of the Precious Crown, Peony|WD=Q64735711}} |religion= {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |native_name= 千家典子 |given_name= {{ill|Noriko|WD=Q7051261}} }} {{nihongo|'''Noriko Senge'''|千家 典子|Senge Noriko|extra= born 22 July 1988}}, formerly {{nihongo|'''Princess Noriko of Takamado'''|典子女王|Noriko Joō}}, is a former member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] and the second daughter of [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] and [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako, Princess Takamado]].{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/asia-pacific/2526599.stm|title=Funeral held for Japan's prince|date=29 November 2002|work=[[BBC Online]]|accessdate=28 April 2011}} She married Kunimaro Senge, a commoner, on 5 October 2014. As a result, she gave up her imperial title and left the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]], as required by law. ==Biography== [[File:Cephalanthera falcata 1.jpg|thumb|right|upright|[[:en:orchid|orchid]]s, designated imperial personal emblem of Noriko]] Princess Noriko was born on 22 July 1988 at Aiiku Hospital in [[:en:Minami-Azabu|Minami-Azabu]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. After her birth, her father, Prince Takamado, chose her name. She went to Gakushuin Kindergarten. After that she attended the prestigious [[:en:Gakushūin|Gakushuin]] School for her primary, junior high, and high school education. In April 2007, Princess Noriko enrolled in the [[:en:Gakushuin University|Gakushuin University]], Faculty of Letters, Department of Psychology, and expressed her intent to pursue studies in the field of [[:en:clinical psychology|clinical psychology]].{{cite web |title=Personal History of Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family |url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/history/history08.html |publisher=[[Imperial Household Agency]] |date=|accessdate=19 May 2012}} In July 2008, she came of age and attended official ceremonies in Japan with the other members of the Imperial Family. She attended official functions at the palace and accompanied her mother to some other official events, as well as attending some on her own. On 20 March 2011, she graduated from Gakushuin University with the [[:en:Bachelor of Arts|B.A.]] degree in [[:en:Psychology|Psychology]]. ===Marriage=== On 27 May 2014, Princess Noriko announced her engagement to {{nihongo|Kunimaro Senge|千家国麿|Senge Kunimaro|extra= born {{Birth date|1973|9|2|df=y}}}}, a [[kannushi]] for the [[Izumo-taisha]] shrine.{{cite news|title=Wedding Bells to Ring for Japanese Princess|url=https://blogs.wsj.com/japanrealtime/2014/05/27/wedding-bells-to-ring-for-japanese-princess/|newspaper=Wall Street Journal Japan}} The two married on 5 October at the shrine.{{cite web|title=Princess Noriko marries son of Izumo Taisha priest, relinquishes royal status|date=5 October 2014 |url=http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/10/05/national/princess-noriko-marries-son-of-izumo-taisha-priest-relinquishes-royal-status/#.VDFoc1e5_y0|accessdate=5 October 2014}}{{cite news|title=Marriage of Her Imperial Highness Princess Noriko of Takamado - Wedding Ceremony at the Izumo Oyasahiro Shrine in Shimane Prefecture|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/gokekkon-h26/e-kekkonsiki-ph.html|work=The Imperial Household Agency|accessdate=16 May 2015}} Senge Kunimaro is the eldest son of Senge Takamasa, the head priest of the Izumo Grand Shrine. The [[:en:Senge family|Senge family]] have been hereditary administrators of the shrine for generations. Though legally commoners today, the heads of the family held the title of [[:en:Baron|Baron]] in the Imperial Japanese peerage (''[[kazoku]]'') before its abolition in 1947. Senge Kunimaro is a second cousin (twice removed) of the present Emperor and a distant cousin twice over of Princess Noriko. He and Noriko are third cousins once removed by their shared descent from Prince [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Kujō Michitaka]], who was the father of the Empress Teimei, Princess Noriko's great-grandmother and Senge Kunimaro's great-great-aunt.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%8d%83%e5%ae%b6%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%87%ba%e9%9b%b2%e5%9b%bd%e9%80%a0%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#kunimaro973|title=千家家（Izumo Province 造家） (Senge genealogy)|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} The couple are also fifth cousins through their shared descent from Marquis [[:en:Nakayama Tadayasu|Nakayama Tadayasu]], the maternal grandfather of the [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]].{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e4%b8%ad%e5%b1%b1%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e7%be%bd%e6%9e%97%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#suketikaq|script-title=ja:中山家（羽林家）|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} By birth, Kunimaro is also related to other members of the imperial family. He is a sixth cousin once removed of his grandmother-in-law, [[:en:Princess Mikasa|Princess Mikasa]], a third cousin twice removed of [[:en:Princess Hitachi|Princess Hitachi]] and a cousin of the late Princesses [[:en:Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|Takamatsu]] and [[:en:Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|Chichibu]] (a second cousin thrice removed and a fourth cousin thrice removed, respectively). After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and [[:en:allowance (money)|allowance]] from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese Imperial Family]] to marry a commoner since the passage of the [[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial Household Law]] in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of [[:en:Sayako Kuroda|Sayako, Princess Nori]], [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Akihito's]] daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle=[[:en:Imperial Highness|Her Imperial Highness]] | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] |3= 3. [[:en:Hisako, Princess Takamado|Hisako Tottori]] |4= 4. [[:en:Takahito, Prince Mikasa|Takahito, Prince Mikasa]] |5= 5. [[:en:Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|The Hon. Yuriko Takagi]] |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō]] |9= 9. [[:en:Empress Teimei|Lady Sadako Kujō]] |10= 10. Masanari Takagi |11= 11. Kuniko Irie |12= 12. Tamesaburo Tottori |13= 13. Hisashi Tottori |14= 14. Jiro Tomoda |15= 15. Moriko Tomoda |16= 16. [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Mutsuhito, Emperor Meiji]] |17= 17. [[:en:Yanagihara Naruko|Lady Naruko Yanagihara]] |18= 18. [[:en:Kujō Michitaka|Prince Kujō Michitaka]] |19= 19. Noma Ikuko |20= 20. Masayoshi Takagi |21= 21. Saneko Matsudaira |22= 22. Tamemori Irie |23= 23. [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[co:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[co:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[sn:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2740324]] Wikidata name: Noriko Senge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Former Japanese princess (born 1988)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2021}} {{Eastern name order|Senge Noriko}} {{Infobox person |name = Noriko Senge |image = File:Princess Noriko 2013.JPG |caption = Noriko in 2013 |birth_name = {{nihongo|Princess Noriko of Takamado|典子女王}} |birth_date = {{birth date|1988|7|22}} |birth_place = {{ill|Aiiku Hospital|WD=Q11073872}} |spouse = {{ill|Kunimaro Senge|WD=Q16963459}} |father = {{ill|Norihito, Prince Takamado|WD=Q10856511}} |mother = {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104}} |relatives = {{ill|Ayako Moriya|WD=Q2379776}}; {{ill|Princess Tsuguko of Takamado|WD=Q2727922}} |gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} |nationality= {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} |occupation= {{ill|aristocrat|WD=Q2478141}} |family= {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452}} |awards= {{ill|The Order of the Precious Crown, Peony|WD=Q64735711}} |religion= {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |native_name= 千家典子 |given_name= {{ill|Noriko|WD=Q7051261}} }} {{nihongo|'''Noriko Senge'''|千家 典子|Senge Noriko|extra= born 22 July 1988}}, formerly {{nihongo|'''Princess Noriko of Takamado'''|典子女王|Noriko Joō}}, is a former member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} and the second daughter of [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] and {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|en|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104|lt=Hisako, Princess Takamado}}.{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/asia-pacific/2526599.stm|title=Funeral held for Japan's prince|date=29 November 2002|work=[[BBC Online]]|accessdate=28 April 2011}} She married Kunimaro Senge, a commoner, on 5 October 2014. As a result, she gave up her imperial title and left the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese Imperial Family}}, as required by law. ==Biography== [[File:Cephalanthera falcata 1.jpg|thumb|right|upright|{{ill|orchid|en|orchid|WD=Q25308|lt=orchid}}s, designated imperial personal emblem of Noriko]] Princess Noriko was born on 22 July 1988 at Aiiku Hospital in {{ill|Minami-Azabu|en|Minami-Azabu|WD=Q11408823|lt=Minami-Azabu}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. After her birth, her father, Prince Takamado, chose her name. She went to Gakushuin Kindergarten. After that she attended the prestigious {{ill|Gakushūin|en|Gakushūin|WD=Q1061900|lt=Gakushuin}} School for her primary, junior high, and high school education. In April 2007, Princess Noriko enrolled in the {{ill|Gakushuin University|en|Gakushuin University|WD=Q1372362|lt=Gakushuin University}}, Faculty of Letters, Department of Psychology, and expressed her intent to pursue studies in the field of {{ill|clinical psychology|en|clinical psychology|WD=Q199906|lt=clinical psychology}}.{{cite web |title=Personal History of Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family |url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/history/history08.html |publisher=[[Imperial Household Agency]] |date=|accessdate=19 May 2012}} In July 2008, she came of age and attended official ceremonies in Japan with the other members of the Imperial Family. She attended official functions at the palace and accompanied her mother to some other official events, as well as attending some on her own. On 20 March 2011, she graduated from Gakushuin University with the {{ill|Bachelor of Arts|en|Bachelor of Arts|WD=Q1765120|lt=B.A.}} degree in {{ill|Psychology|en|Psychology|WD=Q9418|lt=Psychology}}. ===Marriage=== On 27 May 2014, Princess Noriko announced her engagement to {{nihongo|Kunimaro Senge|千家国麿|Senge Kunimaro|extra= born {{Birth date|1973|9|2|df=y}}}}, a [[kannushi]] for the [[Izumo-taisha]] shrine.{{cite news|title=Wedding Bells to Ring for Japanese Princess|url=https://blogs.wsj.com/japanrealtime/2014/05/27/wedding-bells-to-ring-for-japanese-princess/|newspaper=Wall Street Journal Japan}} The two married on 5 October at the shrine.{{cite web|title=Princess Noriko marries son of Izumo Taisha priest, relinquishes royal status|date=5 October 2014 |url=http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/10/05/national/princess-noriko-marries-son-of-izumo-taisha-priest-relinquishes-royal-status/#.VDFoc1e5_y0|accessdate=5 October 2014}}{{cite news|title=Marriage of Her Imperial Highness Princess Noriko of Takamado - Wedding Ceremony at the Izumo Oyasahiro Shrine in Shimane Prefecture|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/gokekkon-h26/e-kekkonsiki-ph.html|work=The Imperial Household Agency|accessdate=16 May 2015}} Senge Kunimaro is the eldest son of Senge Takamasa, the head priest of the Izumo Grand Shrine. The [[:en:Senge family|Senge family]] have been hereditary administrators of the shrine for generations. Though legally commoners today, the heads of the family held the title of {{ill|Baron|en|Baron|WD=Q165503|lt=Baron}} in the Imperial Japanese peerage (''[[kazoku]]'') before its abolition in 1947. Senge Kunimaro is a second cousin (twice removed) of the present Emperor and a distant cousin twice over of Princess Noriko. He and Noriko are third cousins once removed by their shared descent from Prince {{ill|Kujō Michitaka|en|Kujō Michitaka|WD=Q1062364|lt=Kujō Michitaka}}, who was the father of the Empress Teimei, Princess Noriko's great-grandmother and Senge Kunimaro's great-great-aunt.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%8d%83%e5%ae%b6%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%87%ba%e9%9b%b2%e5%9b%bd%e9%80%a0%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#kunimaro973|title=千家家（Izumo Province 造家） (Senge genealogy)|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} The couple are also fifth cousins through their shared descent from Marquis {{ill|Nakayama Tadayasu|en|Nakayama Tadayasu|WD=Q1963991|lt=Nakayama Tadayasu}}, the maternal grandfather of the {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}}.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e4%b8%ad%e5%b1%b1%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e7%be%bd%e6%9e%97%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#suketikaq|script-title=ja:中山家（羽林家）|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} By birth, Kunimaro is also related to other members of the imperial family. He is a sixth cousin once removed of his grandmother-in-law, [[:en:Princess Mikasa|Princess Mikasa]], a third cousin twice removed of [[:en:Princess Hitachi|Princess Hitachi]] and a cousin of the late Princesses {{ill|Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|en|Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|WD=Q467655|lt=Takamatsu}} and {{ill|Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|en|Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|WD=Q3244162|lt=Chichibu}} (a second cousin thrice removed and a fourth cousin thrice removed, respectively). After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and {{ill|allowance (money)|en|allowance (money)|WD=Q2364815|lt=allowance}} from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese Imperial Family}} to marry a commoner since the passage of the {{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial Household Law}} in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of {{ill|Sayako Kuroda|en|Sayako Kuroda|WD=Q229333|lt=Sayako, Princess Nori}}, {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|WD=Q37979|lt=Emperor Akihito's}} daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle={{ill|Imperial Highness|en|Imperial Highness|WD=Q5351551|lt=Her Imperial Highness}} | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] |3= 3. {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|en|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104|lt=Hisako Tottori}} |4= 4. {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Takahito, Prince Mikasa}} |5= 5. {{ill|Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|en|Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|WD=Q1189486|lt=The Hon. Yuriko Takagi}} |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō}} |9= 9. {{ill|Empress Teimei|en|Empress Teimei|WD=Q1197656|lt=Lady Sadako Kujō}} |10= 10. Masanari Takagi |11= 11. Kuniko Irie |12= 12. Tamesaburo Tottori |13= 13. Hisashi Tottori |14= 14. Jiro Tomoda |15= 15. Moriko Tomoda |16= 16. {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Mutsuhito, Emperor Meiji}} |17= 17. [[:en:Yanagihara Naruko|Lady Naruko Yanagihara]] |18= 18. {{ill|Kujō Michitaka|en|Kujō Michitaka|WD=Q1062364|lt=Prince Kujō Michitaka}} |19= 19. Noma Ikuko |20= 20. Masayoshi Takagi |21= 21. Saneko Matsudaira |22= 22. Tamemori Irie |23= 23. Nobuko Yanagihara }} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Senge Noriko}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/activity08.html Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family] at the Imperial Household Agency website {{Japanese princesses}} {{Izumo-taishakyo}} [[en:Noriko Senge]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]] [[azb:نوریکو سنقه]] {{Commons category|Senge Noriko}} [[es:Noriko Senge]] [[fa:سنگه نوریکو]] [[fr:Noriko Senge]] [[gn:Noriko Senge]] [[id:Noriko Senge]] [[it:Noriko Senge]] [[ja:千家典子]] [[ko:센게 노리코]] [[pl:Noriko Senge]] [[pt:Noriko Senge]] [[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]] [[vi:Senge Noriko]] [[zh:千家典子]] {{wikidata link|Q2740324}} [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Gakushuin University alumni]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Nobility from Tokyo]] [[Category:Order of the Precious Crown members]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:People from Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Nobuko Yanagihara }} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Senge Noriko}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/activity08.html Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family] at the Imperial Household Agency website {{Japanese princesses}} {{Izumo-taishakyo}} [[en:Noriko Senge]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]] [[azb:نوریکو سنقه]] {{Commons category|Senge Noriko}} [[es:Noriko Senge]] [[fa:سنگه نوریکو]] [[fr:Noriko Senge]] [[gn:Noriko Senge]] [[id:Noriko Senge]] [[it:Noriko Senge]] [[ja:千家典子]] [[ko:센게 노리코]] [[pl:Noriko Senge]] [[pt:Noriko Senge]] [[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]] [[vi:Senge Noriko]] [[zh:千家典子]] {{wikidata link|Q2740324}} [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Gakushuin University alumni]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Nobility from Tokyo]] [[Category:Order of the Precious Crown members]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:People from Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[co:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[co:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[sn:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2740324]] Wikidata name: Noriko Senge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Former Japanese princess (born 1988)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2021}} {{Eastern name order|Senge Noriko}} {{Infobox person |name = Noriko Senge |image = File:Princess Noriko 2013.JPG |caption = Noriko in 2013 |birth_name = {{nihongo|Princess Noriko of Takamado|典子女王}} |birth_date = {{birth date|1988|7|22}} |birth_place = {{ill|Aiiku Hospital|WD=Q11073872}} |spouse = {{ill|Kunimaro Senge|WD=Q16963459}} |father = {{ill|Norihito, Prince Takamado|WD=Q10856511}} |mother = {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104}} |relatives = {{ill|Ayako Moriya|WD=Q2379776}}; {{ill|Princess Tsuguko of Takamado|WD=Q2727922}} |gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} |nationality= {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} |occupation= {{ill|aristocrat|WD=Q2478141}} |family= {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452}} |awards= {{ill|The Order of the Precious Crown, Peony|WD=Q64735711}} |religion= {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |native_name= 千家典子 |given_name= {{ill|Noriko|WD=Q7051261}} }} {{nihongo|'''Noriko Senge'''|千家 典子|Senge Noriko|extra= born 22 July 1988}}, formerly {{nihongo|'''Princess Noriko of Takamado'''|典子女王|Noriko Joō}}, is a former member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} and the second daughter of [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] and {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|en|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104|lt=Hisako, Princess Takamado}}.{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/asia-pacific/2526599.stm|title=Funeral held for Japan's prince|date=29 November 2002|work=[[BBC Online]]|accessdate=28 April 2011}} She married Kunimaro Senge, a commoner, on 5 October 2014. As a result, she gave up her imperial title and left the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese Imperial Family}}, as required by law. ==Biography== [[File:Cephalanthera falcata 1.jpg|thumb|right|upright|{{ill|orchid|en|orchid|WD=Q25308|lt=orchid}}s, designated imperial personal emblem of Noriko]] Princess Noriko was born on 22 July 1988 at Aiiku Hospital in {{ill|Minami-Azabu|en|Minami-Azabu|WD=Q11408823|lt=Minami-Azabu}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. After her birth, her father, Prince Takamado, chose her name. She went to Gakushuin Kindergarten. After that she attended the prestigious {{ill|Gakushūin|en|Gakushūin|WD=Q1061900|lt=Gakushuin}} School for her primary, junior high, and high school education. In April 2007, Princess Noriko enrolled in the {{ill|Gakushuin University|en|Gakushuin University|WD=Q1372362|lt=Gakushuin University}}, Faculty of Letters, Department of Psychology, and expressed her intent to pursue studies in the field of {{ill|clinical psychology|en|clinical psychology|WD=Q199906|lt=clinical psychology}}.{{cite web |title=Personal History of Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family |url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/history/history08.html |publisher=[[Imperial Household Agency]] |date=|accessdate=19 May 2012}} In July 2008, she came of age and attended official ceremonies in Japan with the other members of the Imperial Family. She attended official functions at the palace and accompanied her mother to some other official events, as well as attending some on her own. On 20 March 2011, she graduated from Gakushuin University with the {{ill|Bachelor of Arts|en|Bachelor of Arts|WD=Q1765120|lt=B.A.}} degree in {{ill|Psychology|en|Psychology|WD=Q9418|lt=Psychology}}. ===Marriage=== On 27 May 2014, Princess Noriko announced her engagement to {{nihongo|Kunimaro Senge|千家国麿|Senge Kunimaro|extra= born {{Birth date|1973|9|2|df=y}}}}, a [[kannushi]] for the [[Izumo-taisha]] shrine.{{cite news|title=Wedding Bells to Ring for Japanese Princess|url=https://blogs.wsj.com/japanrealtime/2014/05/27/wedding-bells-to-ring-for-japanese-princess/|newspaper=Wall Street Journal Japan}} The two married on 5 October at the shrine.{{cite web|title=Princess Noriko marries son of Izumo Taisha priest, relinquishes royal status|date=5 October 2014 |url=http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/10/05/national/princess-noriko-marries-son-of-izumo-taisha-priest-relinquishes-royal-status/#.VDFoc1e5_y0|accessdate=5 October 2014}}{{cite news|title=Marriage of Her Imperial Highness Princess Noriko of Takamado - Wedding Ceremony at the Izumo Oyasahiro Shrine in Shimane Prefecture|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/gokekkon-h26/e-kekkonsiki-ph.html|work=The Imperial Household Agency|accessdate=16 May 2015}} Senge Kunimaro is the eldest son of Senge Takamasa, the head priest of the Izumo Grand Shrine. The [[:en:Senge family|Senge family]] have been hereditary administrators of the shrine for generations. Though legally commoners today, the heads of the family held the title of {{ill|Baron|en|Baron|WD=Q165503|lt=Baron}} in the Imperial Japanese peerage (''[[kazoku]]'') before its abolition in 1947. Senge Kunimaro is a second cousin (twice removed) of the present Emperor and a distant cousin twice over of Princess Noriko. He and Noriko are third cousins once removed by their shared descent from Prince {{ill|Kujō Michitaka|en|Kujō Michitaka|WD=Q1062364|lt=Kujō Michitaka}}, who was the father of the Empress Teimei, Princess Noriko's great-grandmother and Senge Kunimaro's great-great-aunt.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%8d%83%e5%ae%b6%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%87%ba%e9%9b%b2%e5%9b%bd%e9%80%a0%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#kunimaro973|title=千家家（Izumo Province 造家） (Senge genealogy)|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} The couple are also fifth cousins through their shared descent from Marquis {{ill|Nakayama Tadayasu|en|Nakayama Tadayasu|WD=Q1963991|lt=Nakayama Tadayasu}}, the maternal grandfather of the {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}}.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e4%b8%ad%e5%b1%b1%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e7%be%bd%e6%9e%97%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#suketikaq|script-title=ja:中山家（羽林家）|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} By birth, Kunimaro is also related to other members of the imperial family. He is a sixth cousin once removed of his grandmother-in-law, [[:en:Princess Mikasa|Princess Mikasa]], a third cousin twice removed of [[:en:Princess Hitachi|Princess Hitachi]] and a cousin of the late Princesses {{ill|Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|en|Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|WD=Q467655|lt=Takamatsu}} and {{ill|Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|en|Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|WD=Q3244162|lt=Chichibu}} (a second cousin thrice removed and a fourth cousin thrice removed, respectively). After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and {{ill|allowance (money)|en|allowance (money)|WD=Q2364815|lt=allowance}} from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese Imperial Family}} to marry a commoner since the passage of the {{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial Household Law}} in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of {{ill|Sayako Kuroda|en|Sayako Kuroda|WD=Q229333|lt=Sayako, Princess Nori}}, {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|WD=Q37979|lt=Emperor Akihito's}} daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle={{ill|Imperial Highness|en|Imperial Highness|WD=Q5351551|lt=Her Imperial Highness}} | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] |3= 3. {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|en|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104|lt=Hisako Tottori}} |4= 4. {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Takahito, Prince Mikasa}} |5= 5. {{ill|Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|en|Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|WD=Q1189486|lt=The Hon. Yuriko Takagi}} |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō}} |9= 9. {{ill|Empress Teimei|en|Empress Teimei|WD=Q1197656|lt=Lady Sadako Kujō}} |10= 10. Masanari Takagi |11= 11. Kuniko Irie |12= 12. Tamesaburo Tottori |13= 13. Hisashi Tottori |14= 14. Jiro Tomoda |15= 15. Moriko Tomoda |16= 16. {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Mutsuhito, Emperor Meiji}} |17= 17. [[:en:Yanagihara Naruko|Lady Naruko Yanagihara]] |18= 18. {{ill|Kujō Michitaka|en|Kujō Michitaka|WD=Q1062364|lt=Prince Kujō Michitaka}} |19= 19. Noma Ikuko |20= 20. Masayoshi Takagi |21= 21. Saneko Matsudaira |22= 22. Tamemori Irie |23= 23. Nobuko Yanagihara }} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Senge Noriko}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/activity08.html Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family] at the Imperial Household Agency website {{Japanese princesses}} {{Izumo-taishakyo}} [[en:Noriko Senge]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]] [[azb:نوریکو سنقه]] {{Commons category|Senge Noriko}} [[es:Noriko Senge]] [[fa:سنگه نوریکو]] [[fr:Noriko Senge]] [[gn:Noriko Senge]] [[id:Noriko Senge]] [[it:Noriko Senge]] [[ja:千家典子]] [[ko:센게 노리코]] [[pl:Noriko Senge]] [[pt:Noriko Senge]] [[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]] [[vi:Senge Noriko]] [[zh:千家典子]] {{wikidata link|Q2740324}} [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Gakushuin University alumni]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Nobility from Tokyo]] [[Category:Order of the Precious Crown members]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:People from Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and {{ill|allowance (money)|en|allowance (money)|WD=Q2364815|lt=allowance}} from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese Imperial Family}} to marry a commoner since the passage of the {{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial Household Law}} in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of {{ill|Sayako Kuroda|en|Sayako Kuroda|WD=Q229333|lt=Sayako, Princess Nori}}, {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|WD=Q37979|lt=Emperor Akihito's}} daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle={{ill|Imperial Highness|en|Imperial Highness|WD=Q5351551|lt=Her Imperial Highness}} | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. Yuriko Takagi}} |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō}} |9= 9. {{ill|Empress Teimei|en|Empress Teimei|WD=Q1197656|lt=Lady Sadako Kujō}} |10= 10. [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[co:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[co:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]][[azb:نوریکو سنقه]][[sn:commons:Category:Senge Noriko]][[en:Noriko Senge]][[es:Noriko Senge]][[fa:سنگه نوریکو]][[fr:Noriko Senge]][[gn:Noriko Senge]][[id:Noriko Senge]][[it:Noriko Senge]][[ja:千家典子]][[ko:센게 노리코]][[pl:Noriko Senge]][[pt:Noriko Senge]][[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]][[vi:Senge Noriko]][[zh:千家典子]] {{short description|Former Japanese princess (born 1988)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2021}} {{Eastern name order|Senge Noriko}} {{Infobox person |name = Noriko Senge |image = File:Princess Noriko 2013.JPG |caption = Noriko in 2013 |birth_name = {{nihongo|Princess Noriko of Takamado|典子女王}} |birth_date = {{birth date|1988|7|22}} |birth_place = {{ill|Aiiku Hospital|WD=Q11073872}} |spouse = {{ill|Kunimaro Senge|WD=Q16963459}} |father = {{ill|Norihito, Prince Takamado|WD=Q10856511}} |mother = {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104}} |relatives = {{ill|Ayako Moriya|WD=Q2379776}}; {{ill|Princess Tsuguko of Takamado|WD=Q2727922}} |gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} |nationality= {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} |occupation= {{ill|aristocrat|WD=Q2478141}} |family= {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452}} |awards= {{ill|The Order of the Precious Crown, Peony|WD=Q64735711}} |religion= {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |native_name= 千家典子 |given_name= {{ill|Noriko|WD=Q7051261}} }} {{nihongo|'''Noriko Senge'''|千家 典子|Senge Noriko|extra= born 22 July 1988}}, formerly {{nihongo|'''Princess Noriko of Takamado'''|典子女王|Noriko Joō}}, is a former member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} and the second daughter of [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] and {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|en|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104|lt=Hisako, Princess Takamado}}.{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/asia-pacific/2526599.stm|title=Funeral held for Japan's prince|date=29 November 2002|work=[[BBC Online]]|accessdate=28 April 2011}} She married Kunimaro Senge, a commoner, on 5 October 2014. As a result, she gave up her imperial title and left the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese Imperial Family}}, as required by law. ==Biography== [[File:Cephalanthera falcata 1.jpg|thumb|right|upright|{{ill|orchid|en|orchid|WD=Q25308|lt=orchid}}s, designated imperial personal emblem of Noriko]] Princess Noriko was born on 22 July 1988 at Aiiku Hospital in {{ill|Minami-Azabu|en|Minami-Azabu|WD=Q11408823|lt=Minami-Azabu}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. After her birth, her father, Prince Takamado, chose her name. She went to Gakushuin Kindergarten. After that she attended the prestigious {{ill|Gakushūin|en|Gakushūin|WD=Q1061900|lt=Gakushuin}} School for her primary, junior high, and high school education. In April 2007, Princess Noriko enrolled in the {{ill|Gakushuin University|en|Gakushuin University|WD=Q1372362|lt=Gakushuin University}}, Faculty of Letters, Department of Psychology, and expressed her intent to pursue studies in the field of {{ill|clinical psychology|en|clinical psychology|WD=Q199906|lt=clinical psychology}}.{{cite web |title=Personal History of Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family |url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/history/history08.html |publisher=[[Imperial Household Agency]] |date=|accessdate=19 May 2012}} In July 2008, she came of age and attended official ceremonies in Japan with the other members of the Imperial Family. She attended official functions at the palace and accompanied her mother to some other official events, as well as attending some on her own. On 20 March 2011, she graduated from Gakushuin University with the {{ill|Bachelor of Arts|en|Bachelor of Arts|WD=Q1765120|lt=B.A.}} degree in {{ill|Psychology|en|Psychology|WD=Q9418|lt=Psychology}}. ===Marriage=== On 27 May 2014, Princess Noriko announced her engagement to {{nihongo|Kunimaro Senge|千家国麿|Senge Kunimaro|extra= born {{Birth date|1973|9|2|df=y}}}}, a [[kannushi]] for the [[Izumo-taisha]] shrine.{{cite news|title=Wedding Bells to Ring for Japanese Princess|url=https://blogs.wsj.com/japanrealtime/2014/05/27/wedding-bells-to-ring-for-japanese-princess/|newspaper=Wall Street Journal Japan}} The two married on 5 October at the shrine.{{cite web|title=Princess Noriko marries son of Izumo Taisha priest, relinquishes royal status|date=5 October 2014 |url=http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/10/05/national/princess-noriko-marries-son-of-izumo-taisha-priest-relinquishes-royal-status/#.VDFoc1e5_y0|accessdate=5 October 2014}}{{cite news|title=Marriage of Her Imperial Highness Princess Noriko of Takamado - Wedding Ceremony at the Izumo Oyasahiro Shrine in Shimane Prefecture|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/gokekkon-h26/e-kekkonsiki-ph.html|work=The Imperial Household Agency|accessdate=16 May 2015}} Senge Kunimaro is the eldest son of Senge Takamasa, the head priest of the Izumo Grand Shrine. The [[:en:Senge family|Senge family]] have been hereditary administrators of the shrine for generations. Though legally commoners today, the heads of the family held the title of {{ill|Baron|en|Baron|WD=Q165503|lt=Baron}} in the Imperial Japanese peerage (''[[kazoku]]'') before its abolition in 1947. Senge Kunimaro is a second cousin (twice removed) of the present Emperor and a distant cousin twice over of Princess Noriko. He and Noriko are third cousins once removed by their shared descent from Prince {{ill|Kujō Michitaka|en|Kujō Michitaka|WD=Q1062364|lt=Kujō Michitaka}}, who was the father of the Empress Teimei, Princess Noriko's great-grandmother and Senge Kunimaro's great-great-aunt.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%8d%83%e5%ae%b6%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%87%ba%e9%9b%b2%e5%9b%bd%e9%80%a0%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#kunimaro973|title=千家家（Izumo Province 造家） (Senge genealogy)|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} The couple are also fifth cousins through their shared descent from Marquis {{ill|Nakayama Tadayasu|en|Nakayama Tadayasu|WD=Q1963991|lt=Nakayama Tadayasu}}, the maternal grandfather of the {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}}.{{cite web|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e4%b8%ad%e5%b1%b1%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e7%be%bd%e6%9e%97%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#suketikaq|script-title=ja:中山家（羽林家）|website=Reichsarchiv|access-date=17 May 2017|language=ja}} By birth, Kunimaro is also related to other members of the imperial family. He is a sixth cousin once removed of his grandmother-in-law, [[:en:Princess Mikasa|Princess Mikasa]], a third cousin twice removed of [[:en:Princess Hitachi|Princess Hitachi]] and a cousin of the late Princesses {{ill|Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|en|Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu|WD=Q467655|lt=Takamatsu}} and {{ill|Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|en|Setsuko, Princess Chichibu|WD=Q3244162|lt=Chichibu}} (a second cousin thrice removed and a fourth cousin thrice removed, respectively). After the wedding, Princess Noriko lost her imperial status. This change in her status is mandated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Act|Imperial Household Act of 1947]] that requires females of the Imperial Family to relinquish their title from birth, official membership in the Imperial Family, and {{ill|allowance (money)|en|allowance (money)|WD=Q2364815|lt=allowance}} from the state upon marriage to a commoner. At 26 years of age, she became the seventh female member born into the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese Imperial Family}} to marry a commoner since the passage of the {{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial Household Law}} in 1947, and the first member of the family to lose her royal status since the marriage of {{ill|Sayako Kuroda|en|Sayako Kuroda|WD=Q229333|lt=Sayako, Princess Nori}}, {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|WD=Q37979|lt=Emperor Akihito's}} daughter, in 2005. The government decided to bestow a one-time ¥106.75 million allowance on the couple. ==Titles and styles== {{Infobox royal styles | royal name=Princess Noriko of Takamado
(before her marriage)| | image=[[Image:Japanese Crest of Takamado no miya.svg|centre|Takamado no miya mon|120px]] | dipstyle={{ill|Imperial Highness|en|Imperial Highness|WD=Q5351551|lt=Her Imperial Highness}} | offstyle=Your Imperial Highness }} *22 July 1988 – 5 October 2014: ''Her Imperial Highness'' Princess Noriko of Takamado *5 October 2014 – present: ''Mrs.'' Kunimaro Senge ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |1= 1.'''Princess Noriko of Takamado''' |2= 2. [[Norihito, Prince Takamado]] |3= 3. {{ill|Hisako, Princess Takamado|en|Hisako, Princess Takamado|WD=Q448104|lt=Hisako Tottori}} |4= 4. {{ill|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|en|Takahito, Prince Mikasa|WD=Q280229|lt=Takahito, Prince Mikasa}} |5= 5. {{ill|Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|en|Yuriko, Princess Mikasa|WD=Q1189486|lt=The Hon. Yuriko Takagi}} |6= 6. Shigejiro Tottori |7= 7. Fumiko Tomoda |8= 8. {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Yoshihito, Emperor Taishō}} |9= 9. {{ill|Empress Teimei|en|Empress Teimei|WD=Q1197656|lt=Lady Sadako Kujō}} |10= 10. Saneko Matsudaira |22= 22. Tamemori Irie |23= 23. Nobuko Yanagihara }} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Senge Noriko}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/e-about/activity/activity08.html Her Imperial Highness Princess Takamado and her family] at the Imperial Household Agency website {{Japanese princesses}} {{Izumo-taishakyo}} [[en:Noriko Senge]] [[ast:Noriko Senge]] [[azb:نوریکو سنقه]] {{Commons category|Senge Noriko}} [[es:Noriko Senge]] [[fa:سنگه نوریکو]] [[fr:Noriko Senge]] [[gn:Noriko Senge]] [[id:Noriko Senge]] [[it:Noriko Senge]] [[ja:千家典子]] [[ko:센게 노리코]] [[pl:Noriko Senge]] [[pt:Noriko Senge]] [[th:โนริโกะ เซ็งเงะ]] [[vi:Senge Noriko]] [[zh:千家典子]] {{wikidata link|Q2740324}} [[Category:1988 births]] [[Category:Gakushuin University alumni]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Nobility from Tokyo]] [[Category:Order of the Precious Crown members]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:People from Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
